summary: Full IFC4 runtime bundle for modern building-centric models. IFC4 moves IfcProject under IfcContext, keeps the familiar aggregate and containment relations, and adds more explicit slab variants such as IfcSlabElementedCase and IfcSlabStandardCase.
cautions: IfcProject is a subtype of IfcContext in IFC4, so context-root wording is more accurate than older object-root phrasing.
IfcRoof uses PredefinedType in IFC4; do not look for IFC2X3's ShapeType field here.
IfcSlabElementedCase and IfcSlabStandardCase may appear and are worth checking when the user asks about slab variants or visible roof or floor geometry.
Use DB node ids for graph actions and GlobalId values for viewer element actions.
queryHabits: Use id(n) AS node_id for graph seeds and graph reasoning.
Use GlobalId AS global_id for hide, show, and select actions in the viewer.
If a roof action fails directly, inspect related slabs and slab case variants before concluding nothing is renderable.
Use nearby type, property, and material relations to turn weak labels or enum values into human explanations.
Prefer small DISTINCT traversals across aggregates and containment relations rather than broad scans.

IfcProject.summary: Semantic root and context root for the IFC model. Good starting point for graph orientation and project-level questions.
queryPatterns: MATCH (p:IfcProject) RETURN id(p) AS node_id LIMIT 1

IfcBuildingStorey.summary: Spatial structure node used for containment and storey membership.
queryPatterns: MATCH (s:IfcBuildingStorey) RETURN id(s) AS node_id LIMIT 8

IfcRoof.summary: Semantic roof product. Often still a semantic or aggregate parent rather than the actual visible geometry carrier.
queryPatterns: MATCH (r:IfcRoof) RETURN id(r) AS node_id LIMIT 8
MATCH (:IfcRoof)<--(:IfcRelAggregates)-->(slab:IfcSlab) RETURN DISTINCT id(slab) AS node_id LIMIT 16

IfcSlab.summary: General slab product. Often the better viewer-action target for roof/floor geometry questions.
queryPatterns: MATCH (slab:IfcSlab) RETURN id(slab) AS node_id LIMIT 8
MATCH (slab:IfcSlab)<-[r]-(other) RETURN type(r) AS rel, other.declared_entity AS entity, other.Name AS name LIMIT 24

IfcSlabElementedCase.summary: IFC4 slab case variant worth checking when the user asks what kinds of slabs exist or why a slab behaves differently from another slab.

IfcSlabStandardCase.summary: IFC4 slab case variant that often appears in more regular building exports.

IfcRelAggregates.summary: Main decomposition/aggregate relation. Use it for project/site/building/storey hierarchy and for roof-to-slab traversal.

IfcRelContainedInSpatialStructure.summary: Containment relation between products and spatial structure.

IfcRelDefinesByType.summary: Type attachment relation. Good first stop when an element's name or enum value is not enough.

IfcRelDefinesByProperties.summary: Property-set attachment relation. Use it to explain product behavior or role through psets.

IfcRelAssociatesMaterial.summary: Material attachment relation. Useful for composition questions.

projectSeed: MATCH (p:IfcProject) RETURN id(p) AS node_id LIMIT 1
buildingStructure: MATCH (n) WHERE n.declared_entity IN ['IfcProject','IfcSite','IfcBuilding','IfcBuildingStorey'] RETURN id(n) AS node_id LIMIT 24
roofToSlabsGraph: MATCH (:IfcRoof)<--(:IfcRelAggregates)-->(slab:IfcSlab) RETURN DISTINCT id(slab) AS node_id LIMIT 16
slabRelationContext: MATCH (slab:IfcSlab)<-[r]-(other) RETURN type(r) AS rel, other.declared_entity AS entity, other.Name AS name LIMIT 24
